title: Randia subcordata
taxonomy: Randia subcordata is classified under the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family includes approximately 700 genera and 13,000 species of flowering plants. The genus Randia itself is a group of flowering plants native to tropical regions of the Americas. Randia subcordata specifically is native to Central America and is a part of the tropical lowland rainforests in that region. It is a shrub or small tree species within the genus Randia.

intro: Randia subcordata, also known as Randia, is a medicinal plant species native to tropical regions, particularly in Southeast Asia and the Pacific Islands. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and skin infections. Bioactive constituents of Randia subcordata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Randia subcordata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
